Job Title: Senior Data Engineers (GCP, DBT, Vertex AI)
Ad Text:
Job Description:
We are looking for a motivated and technically strong Senior Data Engineer with strong GCP (BigQuery, vertex AI), DBT and SQL experience with 5–15 years of IT experience and a proven track record of working with DBT for the past 1–2 years.

This role involves building and optimizing data pipelines, creating scalable transformation workflows, and enabling reliable data delivery across the organization.

Key Responsibilities:

Design, build, and manage scalable data pipelines using GCP services like BigQuery, Cloud Functions, and Cloud Storage

Develop and maintain transformation logic and data models in DBT

Ensure data quality, performance, and reliability across all pipeline stages

Collaborate with analysts, data scientists, and engineering teams to deliver data solutions

Implement best practices for data engineering, version control, and CI/CD

May be required to lead small team (3-5) engineers for client projects

Requirements:

5–10 years of total IT experience, with 1–2 years focused on DBT and SQL

Hands-on experience with BigQuery, Cloud Composer, and other GCP/Cloud components

Proficient in DBT, including model development, testing, and documentation

Solid SQL and Python skills for data transformation and scripting

Advanced Python programming skills for complex data processing and development tasks

Familiarity with Git and CI/CD tools

Strong problem-solving and communication skills

Australian work authorisation will be considered on a case-by-case basis

Nice to Have:

Experience with data orchestration tools like Apache Airflow

Exposure to BI tools such as Looker, Power BI, or Tableau

Understanding of data governance, security, and compliance principles

Job Title: General Manager, Enterprise Performance and Insights
Ad Text:
The Organisation

The Greater Metropolitan Cemeteries Trust (GMCT) is seeking a strategic and inclusive senior leader to step into the newly created role of General Manager, Enterprise Performance and Insights.

Reporting to the Chief Governance Officer, this role sits at the centre of GMCT’s enterprise planning, performance and governance ecosystem. You will play a critical role in translating the Trust’s Strategic Plan 2025–2027 into integrated planning frameworks, disciplined project governance, high-quality enterprise performance reporting, and robust corporate information and compliance practices.





The Position

As General Manager, you will provide strategic oversight of GMCT’s enterprise-wide planning and performance frameworks, ensuring strong organisational alignment, transparency and decision-making confidence. You will lead and mature the Enterprise Portfolio Management Office (EPMO), embed consistent project governance practices across directorates, and deliver executive, and Board-level insights that support accountability and long-term sustainability.

The role also oversees corporate information and records management frameworks, ensuring compliance, integrity and access to high-quality organisational information, while fostering a culture of collaboration, inclusion and continuous improvement across the Trust.

 

Key responsibilities

Lead enterprise planning and annual business planning cycles aligned to GMCT’s strategic priorities

Oversee enterprise performance reporting and analytics to strengthen organisational insight and accountability

Embed and continuously improve enterprise project governance and portfolio management practices

Drive continued maturity and innovation within the EPMO

Ensure corporate information and records management frameworks meet governance and compliance requirements

Build strong partnerships across directorates to support a cohesive, high-performing organisation

 

Areas of focus

Data, insights and performance

Data, analytics and organisational insight are core to this role. The General Manager is responsible for: 

Overseeing enterprise performance reporting and analytics, ensuring the Executive and Board receive clear, trusted and decision‑useful insights 

Strengthening the quality, consistency and narrative of performance data across strategy, projects and operations 

Translating complex information into practical insights that drive prioritisation, course‑correction and organisational learning 

Ensuring corporate information, records and data governance frameworks support accuracy, accessibility, compliance and confidence in decision‑making 

 

Stakeholder complexity and Board engagement 

This is a high‑influence, high‑visibility role operating across a complex stakeholder landscape. The General Manager works closely with: 

The Executive Leadership Team on enterprise priorities, performance, risk and delivery 

The Board and Board Committees through reporting, assurance and governance processes 

Senior leaders across directorates to align strategy, performance expectations and delivery discipline 

 

Strategic alignment, culture and organisational impact 

This role has a material impact on how the strategy is experienced and delivered across the organisation. The role: 

Drives alignment between strategic intent and day‑to‑day delivery 

Shapes a culture of accountability, transparency and continuous improvement 

Enables consistent enterprise ways of working across planning, prioritisation and project delivery 

Supports leaders to make informed decisions and lead with clarity and confidence 

 

About you

You are a senior enterprise leader with a strong track record in organisational performance, planning, governance or transformation within complex, regulated or public-facing environments. You bring the credibility to engage at Executive and Board level, paired with a collaborative and inclusive leadership style.

You will demonstrate:

Deep experience in enterprise planning, performance reporting and governance frameworks

Strong program, portfolio or EPMO leadership capability

A practical understanding of information governance, compliance and accountability

The ability to translate strategy into actionable frameworks and measurable outcomes

A values-led, inclusive approach to leadership and stakeholder engagement




For more information on the organisation visit: www.gmct.com.au

Applications are required by: 8th May 2026




To Apply - Please visit https://futureleadership.com.au/opportunities/ click 'Quick apply', using reference GMCepi0426 addressing your cover letter and resume to Peter Barber of Future Leadership™, or call 1300 347 437 for further information.

A candidate information pack is available to download by visiting: https://futureleadership.com.au/your-career/opportunities/

For further information on Future Leadership™ and additional opportunities please visit https://futureleadership.com.au

Working at Transurban is different; it's a place where you can see the benefits of your work play out in real life, every day. We create city-sized solutions-building and operating safer, smarter, and more sustainable roads-to solve pressing transport challenges that are slowing cities down.





We're seeking a Senior Manager - Customer Transformation to lead a dedicated function of Product Owners and Analysts, championing customer and employee advocacy, and driving scalable Agile ways of working across our Customer and Technology teams. You'll be at the forefront of transforming our business, transformation initiatives, and building roadmaps that align with Transurban's vision and strategy.



You'll join a team focused on transforming, who are collaborative, ambitious and people focused. As our Senior Manager - Customer Transformation, you'll contribute to lasting and positive changes that shape the future of our cities and communities. It's meaningful, challenging and exciting work.





About the role



As our Senior Manager - Customer Transformation, you will be accountable for driving Agile ways of working across existing and new squads as we scale Agile delivery capability across Customer and Technology and setting the standard for our future vision. You will also be accountable for a team working on our Transformation program. This pivotal role will lead teams working through all phases of the delivery lifecycle from idea through to deliver enhanced customer and employee experiences



Day-to-day, you will:








Own and embed our scaled Agile ways of working across Customer and Technology, with a specific focus on the Customer Retail Business. You will lead the implementation of best practice Agile methodologies, driving Agile maturity, managing the flow of work, and facilitating aligned cadences and ceremonies like Quarterly Planning and Squad of Squads across the portfolio.



Lead and grow a high-performing dedicated function of Product Owners and Analysts, providing regular leadership, mentoring, and coaching to all team members. You will ensure appropriate team structures are in place and aligned with the broader Customer & Technology (C&T) functions, as well as the Transformation program.



Be accountable for bringing the C&T Transformation vision to life, across Customer Retail channels and platforms, with clear linkages to C&T Priorities and committed benefits. This includes developing the vision and purpose for both the function.



Establish and maintain effective relationships with key internal and external stakeholders, communicating progress and program health, influencing outcomes, and supporting the Transformation program's objectives. You will also utilise analytics, UX research, and business intelligence tools to evaluate and monitor performance against KPIs, providing insights to inform decisions.







This role will suit someone with a curious mind and transferable skills and experiences, including:






Product management experience working on mass consumer facing products



Agile delivery experience in a complex and dynamic environment



Leadership and management experience



High level business acumen and experience managing budgets and finances



Excellent communication skills, written (including report writing) and verbal and highly advanced influencing and stakeholder management experience



Experience product development software including Jira and Confluence. Solid familiarity with development methodologies, life cycles, and best practices


If you meet some of these requirements, but not all, we encourage you to submit your application.





With a career at Transurban, you'll enjoy a range of benefits, including:






A range of flexible working and leave options, including the option to purchase an additional six weeks of leave each year.



16 weeks paid parental leave (regardless of gender or carer status), with no waiting period, and Superannuation paid during unpaid leave as well.



Learning and development opportunities to support your career interests.



Health and wellbeing support-access to Headspace and our EAP program, wellness facilities, and more.



Share offers and insurance benefits.



Social activities, community give-back programs, and paid volunteer days.





We embrace diversity with a foundation of safety, wellbeing, and inclusion; where everyone belongs. We treat everyone with fairness, consideration, and respect, regardless of gender, gender identity, marital or family status, sexual orientation, age, ethnicity, religious beliefs, cultural or socio-economic background, perspective, and experience. We strongly encourage applications from candidates of diverse backgrounds and lived experiences, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and people living with disability.





Ready to help us keep cities moving? Apply today.

Job Title: Specialist, Global Category Management - 12 month fixed term
Ad Text:
Company description:



About Newmont

Founded in 1921 Newmont Mining Corporation is one of the largest gold mining companies in the world. Newmont prides itself on safety and embraces diversity, engages employees, fosters innovation, rewards high performance and develops great leaders.

Your impact

Reporting to the Director, Global Category Management and embedded within Newmont's Global Supply Chain Operations team in Australia, in the position of Specialist, Camp and Travel you will:

Develop and implement category and sourcing strategies, negotiate and facilitate favorable commercial agreements, and optimize sourcing plans to support global objectives and maximize cost reduction
Manage global supplier relationships, develop and execute Supplier Relationship Management (SRM) programs, and ensure strong working relationships with suppliers for continuous improvement
Serve as the day-to-day interface between business planning, technical services, and other stakeholders to drive contract value, pricing guidance, and achieve budgetary objectives
Maintain awareness of market conditions, lead times, trends, risks, and emerging technologies, focusing on safety, automation, and technological advancements
Proactively lead and coordinate the selection and handling of suppliers across safety, sustainability, and social performance objectives
Apply and manage the utilization of the Request-to-Pay Standard and Category Management frameworks, ensuring compliance, governance, and alignment with stakeholders
Lead the identification and capture of new savings opportunities, manage non-compliance, support Total Cost Ownership (TCO) initiatives, and ensure savings validation and reporting from each initiative
Implement and support continuous improvement opportunities, execute procurement strategies, policies, and guidelines, and enhance procurement capability through training and knowledge sharing
Lead strategic initiatives and portfolio management programs in synchronization with Integrated Business Planning
Support the GCM framework through governance, processes, data requirements and external partnerships.

Prerequisites for your success in the role

Degree qualification in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Accounting, or a related discipline
Deep expertise in strategic sourcing, category and portfolio management, procurement best practices and advanced negotiation strategies
Strong capability in supplier relationship management, including assessment of supplier and contractor financial health and market trends
Demonstrated experience in mining, life of mine environments and global asset intensive industries
Proven ability to manage complex global projects, including developing and managing comprehensive forecasts and budgets
High proficiency in SAP/SAP Ariba, Oniqua and other enterprise level systems
Advanced skills in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and Word
Experience using data analytics and dashboarding tools - i.e. Power BI and Tableau
Full and unrestricted Australian work rights essential.



Job description:



This full-time equivalent position will be offered on a 12-month fixed term basis and will afford the successful candidate the flexibility to work fully remotely from within Australia.

Only those applicants with unrestricted eligibility to work in Australia will be considered.

Job Title: Director - AI & Cloud $400K-$500k base, super, bonus
Ad Text:
Following sustained growth and continued investment in AI and cloud across ANZ, a global consulting leader is expanding its Google Business Group and has created a mandate for a Director – AI & Cloud to drive enterprise transformation at scale.

This is a front-of-market origination role, focused on opening strategic conversations, shaping transformation agendas, and activating complex programs in partnership with Google Cloud.

You will engage directly with executive stakeholders to redefine how organisations operate in an AI-first world, moving beyond technology adoption into true business reinvention.

Why This Role Stands Out
Create, don’t inherit – pure origination focus with autonomy to build strategic pipeline
Strategic alignment with Google Cloud across AI, data and cloud platforms
Operate at C-suite and board level across ANZ enterprise
Work within a fully integrated model – strategy through to execution
Influence high-value, multi-year transformation programs
Direct exposure to ANZ and global leadership
Be part of a growth engine where AI is central to every client conversation
What You’ll Be Responsible For
Originate and shape complex enterprise transformation opportunities
Lead senior client conversations across AI, cloud and operating model change
Frame business-led value propositions, linking technology to measurable outcomes
Build and expand joint opportunities with Google Cloud stakeholders
Qualify and structure early-stage deals, guiding them through to execution
Mobilise multi-disciplinary teams to support pursuit strategy and delivery alignment
About the Organisation

A global leader in enterprise transformation, bringing together strategy, consulting, technology, and operations into a single integrated model designed for the Age of AI.
The Google Business Group is a core strategic pillar, combining deep industry expertise with leading AI and cloud capabilities to help organisations unlock value at scale.

What You Bring
Track record of originating and shaping large, complex enterprise deals
Background in consulting, advisory, or enterprise technology environments
Established ANZ enterprise network (Sydney/Melbourne)
Strong credibility with C-suite and senior decision-makers
Ability to connect AI and cloud capability to business transformation outcomes
Commercial mindset with a focus on growth, influence, and execution
This is a rare opportunity to step into a true market-defining role, shaping how AI and cloud are applied across enterprise organisations in ANZ.

The Role:
Are you passionate about turning data into a real-world impact? Join us as a Senior Data Engineer and help lead a new wave of analytics innovation. You'll be a key part of a dedicated project team delivering high-value, MVP-driven solutions. This role is responsible for advancing our data science practices, from model development to deployment and governance. 
 
What's in it for you? 

Build and deploy statistical models and advanced analytics to solve real business challenges
Influence and uplift our end-to-end data science capability
Work with modern Azure-based platforms
Collaborate with talented teams across analytics, business intelligence, actuarial, data engineering, and business departments
Be part of a values-led organisation with a strong purpose and community focus
Flexible hybrid work arrangement with work from home and in-office
 
Skills & Experience:

For Senior positions - Minimum 7 years' experience as a Data Engineer / Analytics Engineer in a commercial setting.
Bachelor's or higher degree in computer science, information systems, data/analytics engineering, or a related quantitative field.
Proven ability to design, build, and productionise scalable ELT pipelines and analytics datasets (batch and streaming) using modern tooling.
Experience maturing data platforms and engineering practices, including standards for modelling, testing, code review, CI/CD, and governance.
Expertise with Google Cloud data stack—e.g. BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Dataform or dbt (for ELT), Cloud Composer (Airflow), Dataflow/Beam, Pub/Sub, Cloud Run—and strong familiarity with Looker semantic modelling (LookML).
Experience with Azure platforms is desirable. (Data Factories, Fabric Services)
Experience with Microsoft Power BI reports and generating on-demand reports. 
Proficient with modern developer workflows: Git, GitHub/GitLab/Cloud Build CI/CD, Infrastructure-as-Code (e.g., Terraform), notebooks where appropriate.
Strong SQL (performance tuning, partitioning/clustering) and dimensional/data-vault modelling skills.
Highly proficient in Python for data engineering (packaging, testing, virtual environments); familiarity with R an advantage where needed for analytics handoffs.
Solid understanding of security, cost optimisation, observability (logging/metrics/alerts), and data quality frameworks (tests, CI checks, SLAs/SLIs).
 
Highly Desirable  

Experience in Insurance or Banking 
Power BI skills 
Microsoft Azure Certifications 
 
The Company:
Established since 1953 offering stability and security, a great place to grow and develop your career. Having a reputation for excellence you will be able to utilise your skills on challenging and meaningful projects, supported by other subject matter experts within our own collaborative and supportive team.  You will be joining a team from various walks of life accompanied by a broad range of skillsets in this highly collaborative environment where teamwork is our most highly valued asset.  
 
It is essential that you demonstrate in your career, proven experience in your field of expertise. We need forward thinkers, individuals to provide substantiated innovative thinking and solutions to the business.
 
Please note: A criminal background check and written assessment will make up part of the recruitment process.

Due to continuous growth, we have a new opportunity within our Capital Markets, Australia department. This role will support the origination, structuring, pricing, execution, and distribution of syndicated loan transactions across both underwritten and non‑underwritten deals. The position works closely with stakeholders across Corporate Banking, Structured | Project Finance and other front office department; and plays an active role in supporting senior team members in developing and maintaining strong relationships across SMBC, as well as with bank and non‑bank investors in the Australian and Asia Pacific markets.

Reporting to the Executive Director, this position will be responsible for:

Assist with origination, structuring, pricing, underwriting, execution, bookrunning, sell‑down, and distribution of syndicated transactions.
Prepare credit analysis, liquidity analysis, sell‑down strategies, underwriting papers, term sheets, offer letters, financing proposals, and information memoranda.
Support internal and external reporting, marketing materials, publicity materials, and management‑requested research (including loan market data analysis etc).
Coordinate syndication processes including bank meetings, presentations, secondary loan trading, documentation and due diligence reviews, and liaison with legal counsel and third‑party advisors.
Contribute to ESG‑related workstreams, including pitching, execution, reporting, and external engagement.
Collaborate with Corporate Banking and Structured Finance teams to support client coverage and generate MLAB syndication opportunities.
Work closely with regional and global teams to identify new investor opportunities and refer debt transactions appropriately.
Uphold and enhance SMBC's reputation through timely, efficient execution and a strong "Team SMBC / Team Sydney" approach.
Preparing underwriting papers in conjunction with senior managers to present to Capital Markets Group, SFDA and CDAPD.

To succeed in this role, you will have:

3-5 years of banking experience in corporate banking and/or a syndication role, preferably within a major Australian bank.
Experience in loan structuring and negotiating terms and conditions with clients, credit teams, and legal counsel.
Established relationships with clients and banking counterparts in the Australian market (preferred but not a requirement).
Well‑developed interpersonal and influencing capabilities, with experience managing key internal and external stakeholders.
Strong time management and organisational skills, with the ability to manage competing priorities.
Sound sales and negotiation skills, or a demonstrated willingness to develop these through on‑the‑job experience and training.
Ability to deliver high‑quality presentations to senior internal stakeholders and external clients.
Strong written and verbal business communication skills.
High level of accuracy and attention to detail.
University qualification in Banking, Finance, or a related discipline.

SMBC: where you can Be Yourself, Make a Difference and Build Your Career.

This organisation is a publicly owned utilities provider delivering critical services to the Canberra community and surrounding region.
 
They are currently undergoing a significant digital and data transformation, positioning data as a key enabler of future service delivery
 
The Opportunity

As part of this transformation, Leadership & People Enablement has been identified as a strategic priority, with workforce planning playing a key role in enabling this.

This is a greenfield initiative, with no existing workforce planning capability in place. An external consulting agency has recently delivered the strategy and frameworks the focus now is on building the capability and bringing it to life.

To support this, the team is bringing in a data-led Power BI Analyst to shape how workforce insights are delivered across the business.

You’ll be supported by a highly experienced, positive and engaging manager with deep SME knowledge in workforce planning who will you give you the business context while you focus on the data. 
 
The Role

This is a hands-on data role focused on building Power BI dashboards and shaping workforce insights for this. You’ll work across multiple data sources from Aurion (including legacy) & ELMO to build dashboards and reporting that inform workforce decisions at an operational and executive level.

Key responsibilities include:

Build dashboards and reports in Power BI to support workforce planning
Extract, clean and wrangle data from multiple systems using Excel & Power Query
Translate data into clear insights to support business decision-making
The Ideal Person

This role will suit an experienced Data Analyst (3+ years) who has worked with workforce/HR data or is interested in learning this space rather than a workforce planner who uses dashboards.

You’ll bring experience in:

Power BI (intermediate or above), including DAX
Cleaning and wrangling data using Excel and Power Query
Presenting insights to a wide range of non-technical stakeholders
Workforce/HR data (or a strong interest in learning)
Salary / Rate

Full-time (36.05 hours/week) labour hire contract through to February 2027
Estimated rate: $90/hr – $105/hr + 12% Super

To apply online, please click on the appropriate link.
 
Alternatively, for a confidential discussion, please contact Lucas Walker on 0483 911 561 quoting ref no. 21527

About the job Senior Business Analyst

We are Fujitsu

We use technology to make happier lives. We are a global leader in technology and business solutions that transform organisations and the world around us. We have a long heritage of bringing innovation and expertise, continuously working to contribute to the growth of society and our customers.

About the role:

RFQ Opportunity

2-3 months Contract

Melbourne based

Hybrid

Role Overview

The Senior Business Analyst / Systems Analyst will provide specialist analytical and functional analysis services to support the discovery, design, and delivery of system solutions. The role will work closely with business stakeholders, product owners, and technical teams to translate business needs into clearly defined, testable requirements and functional designs.

The engagement will be delivered using Agile methodologies and will involve active participation across the full solution lifecycle, including requirements elicitation, process re‑engineering, system integration specification, solution validation, and User Acceptance Testing (UAT).

Mandatory Requirements

Applicants must meet the following mandatory criteria:

Minimum 3 years of proven system solution analytical experience delivering business and functional analysis for ICT or enterprise system implementations.

Demonstrated experience working on complex system solutions in structured and Agile delivery environments.

Key Responsibilities

Business & Functional Analysis

Provide Agile business analysis and functional analysis services across multiple project phases.

Elicit, analyse, validate, and document business and functional requirements using Agile techniques.

Develop and maintain:

User stories and acceptance criteria

Functional design documentation

Wireframes, screen mock-ups, and prototypes where required

Stakeholder Engagement & Facilitation

Engage and negotiate effectively with business stakeholders, end users, product owners, and technical teams to achieve agreed outcomes.

Lead and facilitate:

Requirements workshops

Solution design workshops

Process mapping and validation sessions

Act as a trusted liaison between business and delivery teams to ensure a shared understanding of requirements and solution intent.

Process Re‑engineering

Analyse and document current‑state (as‑is) and future‑state (to‑be) business processes.

Develop process flows and models to support process improvement and system optimisation.

Identify opportunities for process efficiency, automation, and system alignment.

System Integration & Solution Design

Contribute to and document system integration specifications, ensuring alignment across interfacing applications and platforms.

Support solution design by ensuring business requirements are traceable through to functional and technical solutions.

Solution Validation & UAT Support

Support solution showcases, demonstrations, and walkthroughs with stakeholders.

Assist with:

User Acceptance Testing (UAT) planning

Test scenario and test case development

Defect clarification and resolution support

Ensure delivered solutions meet approved business requirements and acceptance criteria.

Key Skills & Experience

Essential

Proven experience delivering Agile business analysis / functional analysis services.

Strong expertise in requirements documentation, including user stories, wireframes, and functional specifications.

Demonstrated experience leading stakeholder workshops and discussions.

Strong negotiation, communication, and facilitation skills.

Experience in process mapping and business process re‑engineering.

Experience contributing to system integration specifications.

Hands‑on experience supporting solution showcases and User Acceptance Testing (UAT).

Desirable

Experience working in multi‑vendor or complex enterprise environments.

Familiarity with Agile delivery frameworks (e.g. Scrum, Kanban).

Strong documentation and presentation skills.

Deliverables

Business and functional requirements documentation

User stories and acceptance criteria

Process flows (as‑is / to‑be)

Functional design artefacts and system integration inputs

UAT support materials and solution validation output

Join Equifax, a global leader in data, analytics, and information services, and play a pivotal role in shaping the regulatory landscape impacting our business. As a stand-alone Government Relations Senior Advisor, you will be an integral part of our Strategy, Growth & Innovation team, working at the forefront of engagement with government stakeholders. This is an exciting opportunity to leverage your deep understanding of political environments to advocate for Equifax's strategic objectives and contribute to our continued success in the Australian market.

This role can be based in either our North Sydney or Melbourne offices.


What you'll do:

Develop and execute a comprehensive government relations strategy aligned with Equifax's business goals.

Cultivate and maintain strong relationships with key government officials at federal and state levels.

Proactively monitor and analyse legislative and regulatory reforms, assessing their potential impact on Equifax.

Articulate Equifax's position on relevant policy issues through effective advocacy and communication.

Prepare high-quality written communications for government bodies, including submissions and policy briefs.

Represent Equifax at industry events, conferences, and meetings with government stakeholders.

Collaborate closely with internal teams (Legal, Communications, Business Units) to ensure a unified approach to government relations initiatives.

Build and maintain coalitions with other organisations to advance shared policy objectives.


What you'll need:

Extensive experience in government relations, public policy, or a related field.

A strong understanding of Australian legislative and regulatory processes at both federal and state levels.

An established network of relationships with government officials and their advisors.

Exceptional written and verbal communication, interpersonal, and advocacy skills.

Proven ability to analyse complex policy issues, identify potential impacts, and develop effective advocacy strategies.

Demonstrated experience working collaboratively with diverse internal and external stakeholders.

A Bachelor's Degree in Political Science, Public Policy, Law, or a related field. Advanced degree preferred.


What could set you apart:

An understanding of the financial services industry and data privacy regulations in Australia.

A proven track record of successfully influencing policy outcomes and achieving advocacy goals.

Demonstrated experience in building and leading successful coalitions with industry partners.

Exceptional negotiation and influencing skills, with the ability to build consensus among diverse groups.

We’re partnering with a leading organisation to deliver a key Azure Virtual Desktop (AVD) optimisation initiative and are seeking an experienced End-User Computing (EUC) Business Analyst to play a pivotal role.

This is a high-impact opportunity to shape how end users interact with modern desktop environments, bringing clarity and structure to a complex and highly visible program.

As the EUC Business Analyst, you will work closely with business stakeholders and technical teams to define and optimise AVD user profiles across a cohort of 150+ users.

You’ll analyse current state usage, define future state requirements, and ensure all technical inputs are clearly documented for infrastructure teams to design, build, and test fit-for-purpose virtual desktop environments.
  
Key Responsibilities:

Engage with stakeholders to understand and document user needs, applications, and use cases
Analyse current state environments and map requirements across AVD profiles
Define future state user journeys and optimal desktop experiences
Classify and segment users into appropriate AVD profiles
Elicit and document functional and non-functional requirements
Translate business needs into clear technical requirements for engineers and architects
Define application access, packaging, and deployment requirements
Establish performance, availability, and user experience expectations
Work with infrastructure teams on:
Identity integration with Azure Active Directory
Access models and conditional access policies
Endpoint management and device strategies
Define interim hybrid desktop environments during migration
Support change, adoption, and communication activities
Assess downstream impacts to service desk and support models
About You
Proven experience as a Business Analyst within End-User Computing (EUC) or Digital Workplace environments
Strong experience working on AVD, VDI, or desktop transformation programs
Excellent stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to manage highly visible business groups
Skilled in eliciting and documenting both functional and non-functional requirements
Strong analytical capability with the ability to structure and organise complex data sets
Experience translating business needs into technical delivery outcomes
Familiarity with Azure Active Directory, identity, access management, and endpoint management

Supp is a fast-growing jobs platform connecting over 200,000 hospitality professionals with more than 8,000 independent venues across Australia and the US. As we continue to grow, we're looking for talented people to join us and help shape the future of hospitality.

We're looking for an Analytics Engineer to work within our ML and Analytics team - someone skilled at helping others extract meaningful insight from data and make a real impact on the future of our company.

In this position, you'll work across our BigQuery Data Mesh and BI systems, helping create and maintain a core suite of reports, and maintaining the DBT pipelines that fuel our BI, and ML systems. You'll also help drive our Data Platform, ensuring that our data is secure, high quality, and ready to be accessed efficiently to guide business decisions.

We are not looking for an Excel specialist, but someone with experience with fully-fledged Data Platforms and is comfortable with BI systems such as Data Studio, or Tableau.

About the platform

Supp is a dynamic two-sided marketplace connecting businesses and hospitality professionals in real time.

We use data-driven matching to pair the right candidates with the right opportunities.

More than just a jobs board, we have created a platform that offers management of worker profiles, business listings, payments, reviews & ratings, administration and community.

We've only scratched the surface of what's possible. Our growth has given us a clear view of the huge opportunities ahead - and we're building fast to make them happen.

The way we work
Size

As a small team, we hire productive, capable people so that we can stay lean and efficient. Fewer meetings, fewer committees. More time building for our customers!

Teamwork

As a company we strive to make data-driven decisions. You'll collaborate across the business to deliver insights that shape our product, and enable confident decisions

Empowerment

As a team that values each individual's input, we want everyone to bring their ideas to the table. Your expertise will be invaluable in shaping our data platform, and reporting systems

Location

We are a fully distributed team. While we prefer candidates within 2–3 hours of the Australia/Melbourne timezone for smoother teamwork, exceptional applicants from further afield are welcome to apply.

Part-time friendly

Have kids or want to work less and play more? We're open to people who want to work between 3 to 5 days per week.

Successful candidates
What You'll Do

Build and document production-grade ELT data pipelines, following a Medallion architecture

Maintain a core set of BI reports, enabling data-driven decisions by the business

Collaborate with Data Scientists, Engineers to deliver high-impact data products

Help guide our Data Platform, Data Governance, and Data Catalogue tooling. Ensuring data is accessible, secure, high quality, and in an efficient structure

Work with the wider Engineering Team to ensure that data is recorded, and stored efficiently in Iceberg formats

Help improve our Data Platform to bring improved federated data access, empowering the company to derive data-driven insights without waiting

Our existing Data Mesh architecture is hosted on GCP, utilizing Bigquery, and DBT Pipelines, interfacing with downstream ML Pipelines, and Looker Studio Reports

The Opportunity

This is an opportunity to step into a senior advisory leadership role within the Department of Education, working on complex, high-impact initiatives that improve how the system operates and delivers outcomes.

As Director, Business Solutions and Advice, you will lead a small, capable team delivering financial insight, business improvement and strategic advisory services across the Department. The work is varied, often ambiguous and requires both structured thinking and practical delivery.

What You’ll Be Doing

·         Leading advisory engagements from problem definition through to implementation

·         Partnering with senior stakeholders to assess performance and identify opportunities

·         Overseeing financial analysis, modelling and reporting

·         Managing multiple workstreams and delivering high-quality outcomes

·         Building team capability and fostering a collaborative, high-performing environment

What You Bring

·         Experience in consulting, advisory, audit or business improvement

·         Strong stakeholder engagement and influencing capability

·         Ability to operate in complex environments with competing priorities

·         A track record of delivering practical outcomes

·         Leadership experience, including developing teams

·         Government experience is valued but not essential.




Why This Role

·         Work on meaningful challenges within a purpose-driven organisation

·         Influence decision making at a senior level

·         See work through to implementation and impact

·         Gain exposure across a large and complex system

Help shape what’s next in data, AI, and advisory within Altis Consulting.   We’re looking for a Principal Consultant to join the team and play a key role in building our Victorian success.  

This is more than a delivery role. We’re looking for someone who can bring strong technical capability, commercial thinking, and a modern advisory mindset to help clients solve real business problems with data. 

Join a growing team that values curiosity, collaboration, and practical innovation. You will be someone who is excited by evolving technology, comfortable navigating ambiguity, and motivated by creating solutions that are not only clever, but sustainable, governed, and valuable in the real world. 

In this role 

You will work closely with clients to understand their challenges, shape solutions, and deliver high-impact outcomes across modern data platforms and AI-enabled environments. 

Your role will include: 

Lead the design and delivery of modern data and analytics solutions 
Partner with clients to translate business problems into practical, scalable strategies 
Provide trusted advisory support, recommendations, and technical leadership 
Work across platforms such as Snowflake, Databricks, and Microsoft Fabric 
Support the development of governed, sustainable, and future-ready data solutions 
Mentor other team members, share expertise, and help foster a high-performing, collaborative team environment  
Engage confidently with stakeholders across both technical and non-technical backgrounds  

What you will bring 

We’re looking for someone who combines strong foundational skills with a modern mindset. 

Lets talk experience 

You will bring experience in consulting, advisory, or client-facing delivery roles within data and analytics (No specific industry) 
Strong capability across modern data platforms, particularly Snowflake (essential), Databricks, and/or Fabric (desirable) 
Apply strong data engineering and data modelling capability to build scalable, sustainable, and well-governed solutions that turn complex data into practical business value 
Exposure to AI- solutions, or a clear understanding of how AI can be applied responsibly in enterprise environments, is a bonus.  
The ability to solve real-world business challenges, not just build technical outputs 
Confidence in making recommendations, influencing stakeholders, and leading conversations with clients 
A strong understanding of how to deliver solutions that are scalable, practical, and well governed

Added Bonus 

Previous exposure to modern AI-assisted delivery tools (GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, Cursor, Snowflake Cortex Code or similar) and the judgment to use them responsibly to accelerate delivery while maintaining quality. 

The mindset that matters 

Technical capability is important, but it’s only part of the picture. 

We’re especially interested in people who demonstrate: 

A growth mindset 
Curiosity and a genuine interest in new and emerging technologies 
Resilience and adaptability in changing environments 
Strong collaboration and communication skills 
The confidence to challenge thinking, offer insight, and add value beyond delivery 
A practical, solutions-focused approach 

What’s in it for you 

Meaningful work within data, AI, and business advisory 
The chance to help shape client outcomes, team culture, and market presence 
A collaborative environment that values innovation, learning, and impact 
The opportunity to work with modern platforms and forward-thinking clients

Ad Text:
The Department of Education is seeking a Director, Business Solutions and Advice to lead a high-performing advisory function within the Finance, Procurement and Facilities Branch.
This role sits at the centre of complex, high-impact work, partnering with senior leaders across the Department to improve performance, solve critical challenges and support informed decision making.
You will lead a team delivering financial, analytical and business advisory services across a diverse portfolio of initiatives.

About the Department of Education:  

Working for the Queensland Department of Education means joining an organisation that values its people and promotes leadership and innovation. Be part of an environment that respects professionalism and diversity, offers training and development opportunities and embraces flexible careers and work-life balance. Find out more about working with us.  

Key Highlights:

Lead high-impact work across one of Queensland's largest and most complex departments.
Partner directly with senior leaders to shape decisions and drive improvement.
Deliver outcomes that move beyond analysis into implementation and impact.

About You:

You bring a background in consulting, advisory, audit or business improvement and are comfortable operating in complex environments.

You will demonstrate:

Strong stakeholder engagement and influencing capability at a senior level.
The ability to navigate ambiguity and translate insight into action.
A track record of delivering outcomes, not just recommendations.
Sound judgement and decision-making capability.
Experience leading and developing high-performing teams.
Experience within government or large organisations is highly regarded.

Why Join:

This role offers the opportunity to work on meaningful, system-level challenges within a purpose-driven environment. You will gain broad exposure across financial performance, operational improvement and strategic initiatives while contributing to outcomes that have a tangible and lasting impact.

How to Apply:

Attach a brief resume including contact details for 2 referees (referees should have an understanding of your relevant work history) ;
Attach a 2-page written statement in response to the attached role description.  

To be considered, please click apply and you will be redirected to OnTalent recruitment agency.  For a confidential discussion, contact: Ashleigh McMillan | 07 3305 5800.  

We welcome all applicants to share any support needed to ensure our recruitment process is inclusive.  

Applications remain current for 12 months from the closing date and may be considered for appointment to identical or similar vacancies within the Department.  

Applications from recruitment agencies will not be accepted.

The role

Passionate about leading high‑impact machine learning initiatives and delivering scalable, production‑ready AI solutions?

Motivated to mentor and grow a team of ML and data engineers while remaining hands‑on with advanced model development and deployment?

Inspired to join a collaborative Data & AI organisation that values innovation, technical excellence, and continuous learning?

As our new Machine Learning Engineer – Team Lead, you will play a pivotal role in leading the design and delivery of machine learning solutions end‑to‑end, while providing technical leadership and mentorship to a small, high‑performing engineering team. You will bridge business objectives with advanced ML techniques, guide architectural decisions, and ensure models are production‑ready, scalable, and continuously improved.

Key responsibilities will include:

Lead and mentor a team of ML and Data Engineers, providing technical guidance, regular feedback, and supporting professional development.
Own the end‑to‑end lifecycle of machine learning initiatives, translating business requirements into well‑defined ML problem statements, technical solutions, and delivery plans.
Design, build, train, and evaluate advanced machine learning models, and contribute to architectural decisions for scalable ML pipelines and cloud‑based deployments (Azure preferred).
Partner with MLOps and DevOps teams to implement CI/CD pipelines, model versioning, monitoring, retraining, and production support best practices.
Collaborate cross‑functionally with Data Engineering, Software Development, Sales & Pre‑Sales, and leadership stakeholders, while staying current with emerging ML technologies and driving adoption where valuable.

What we need to see from you
Proven experience delivering machine learning solutions into production environments.
Strong technical expertise in Python and ML frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, and Scikit‑learn.
Solid understanding of end‑to‑end ML pipelines, data preprocessing, feature engineering, model evaluation, and monitoring.
Experience working with cloud platforms (Azure preferred), Docker, Kubernetes, and MLOps tooling (e.g., MLflow, Airflow).
Ability to balance technical depth with leadership responsibilities and commercial awareness and having experience working within agile delivery environments and across multiple stakeholder groups

Job Function

Software & Cloud
